proposed_hypotheses.text: The ordinal feature feature_051 (levels 0,1,2) is inversely associated with objective_response, such that each higher level of feature_051 corresponds to a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.code: import statsmodels.api as sm; X = sm.add_constant(df['feature_051'].astype(float)); m = sm.Logit(df['objective_response'], X).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Strong monotone inverse association: response rates 21.08% (level 0, n=17557), 15.70% (level 1, n=24962), 11.05% (level 2, n=7481). Logistic regression: log-OR = -0.3749 per unit increase in feature_051 (OR 0.687), p = 1.7e-93.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_013 == 1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with feature_013 == 0.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 15.40% with feature_013=1 (n=32365) vs 19.64% with feature_013=0 (n=17635). Risk difference -4.25 pp; OR 0.744; chi-square p = 1.1e-33.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_067 == 1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with feature_067 == 0.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 14.36% with feature_067=1 (n=12584) vs 17.75% with feature_067=0 (n=37416). Risk difference -3.39 pp; OR 0.777; chi-square p = 2.0e-18.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous feature feature_011 are associated with lower probability of objective_response (i.e. negative slope on the log-odds scale).

analyses.result_summary: Quartile RRs: 18.5% (Q1, range 0-3), 16.3% (Q2/Q3, 3-6.4), 14.2% (Q4, >6.4). Univariate logistic: log-OR = -0.0385 per unit (OR 0.962), p = 1.8e-32. Strongly negative continuous association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_006 == 1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with feature_006 == 0.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 18.19% with feature_006=1 (n=14258) vs 16.38% with feature_006=0 (n=35742). Risk difference +1.82 pp; OR 1.136; chi-square p = 1.0e-6. Modest but highly significant positive predictor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_007 == 1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with feature_007 == 0.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 17.54% with feature_007=1 (n=22371) vs 16.37% with feature_007=0 (n=27629). Risk difference +1.16 pp; OR 1.086; chi-square p = 5.9e-4. Small but significant positive predictor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_039 == 1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with feature_039 == 0.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 17.39% with feature_039=1 (n=25008) vs 16.40% with feature_039=0 (n=24992). Risk difference +0.99 pp; OR 1.073; chi-square p = 3.4e-3. Small positive effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous feature feature_099 are associated with higher probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic: log-OR = +0.091 per unit (OR 1.095), p = 1.4e-4. Mean of feature_099 in responders 3.821 vs non-responders 3.798 (small but consistent positive association).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous feature feature_063 are associated with lower probability of objective_response (negative slope).

analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic: log-OR = -0.00487 per unit (OR 0.995), p = 6.9e-4. Mean feature_063 in responders 5.72 vs non-responders 6.09; negative direction confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Smoking status (feature_057: never / former / current) is associated with differential objective_response rates; specifically, never-smokers will have a different response rate than ever-smokers.

analyses.result_summary: Response rates: never 16.38% (n=7459), former 16.85% (n=27567), current 17.23% (n=14974). Chi-square p = 0.27. NOT significant - smoking status by itself does not predict response in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Histology (feature_043: adenocarcinoma vs squamous) differs in objective_response, with squamous histology associated with a different (likely higher, given common ICI patterns) response rate than adenocarcinoma.

analyses.result_summary: Adenocarcinoma RR 16.71% (n=36099) vs squamous RR 17.37% (n=13901). Risk difference +0.66 pp (squamous higher). Chi-square p = 0.078. Borderline / not significant; direction matches squamous-higher hypothesis but magnitude is small.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Self-reported race/ethnicity (feature_123) is associated with differences in objective_response rates across the five categories (white, black, hispanic, asian, other).

analyses.result_summary: Response rates: white 16.55% (n=32607), black 17.76% (n=6008), hispanic 17.15% (n=7392), asian 17.72% (n=2979), other 18.54% (n=1014). Chi-square p = 0.045. Borderline significant overall; non-white groups have slightly higher RR than white but differences are small (range ~16.5-18.5%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Insurance type (feature_005: medicare/medicaid/private/uninsured) is associated with differential objective_response rates.

analyses.result_summary: Response rates: medicaid 16.13% (n=6999), medicare 16.85% (n=21178), private 17.20% (n=19823), uninsured 17.00% (n=2000). Chi-square p = 0.23. NOT significant; private slightly higher than medicaid (difference ~1.1 pp) but no clear gradient.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patient age (feature_078, range 30-90 years) is associated with objective_response, with older patients having a different response rate than younger patients.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic: log-OR = 0.00051 per year (OR 1.0005), p = 0.67. RR by age stratum: <55 16.95%, 55-65 17.02%, 65-75 16.61%, >75 17.17%. Age has essentially no effect on response in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression including all binary, ordinal, continuous and categorical features, the effects identified univariately for feature_051, feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, feature_006, feature_007 and feature_039 will remain independently statistically significant predictors of objective_response in the same direction.

analyses.code: X = sm.add_constant(pd.concat([binaries_df, scaled_continuous, multilevel, dummies], axis=1)); sm.Logit(df['objective_response'], X).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logit (all 77 binary, 35 continuous, multilevel and categorical features): feature_051 coef=-0.382 p=9.4e-96; feature_013 coef=-0.302 p=1.6e-34; feature_011 coef=-0.153 (per SD) p=2.1e-33; feature_067 coef=-0.261 p=3.1e-19; feature_006 coef=+0.131 p=1.6e-6; feature_099 coef=+0.047 (per SD) p=1.0e-4; feature_063 coef=-0.046 (per SD) p=5.2e-4; feature_007 coef=+0.081 p=8.9e-4; feature_039 coef=+0.078 p=1.2e-3. All seven priority features remain independently significant in the predicted direction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive multiplicative interaction between feature_006 and feature_039 on objective_response: the joint presence of feature_006=1 and feature_039=1 produces a response rate that exceeds what would be predicted from the sum of their individual marginal effects.

analyses.result_summary: Joint cross-table response rates: 16.27% (006=0,039=0), 16.48% (006=0,039=1), 16.72% (006=1,039=0), 19.67% (006=1,039=1). Logit interaction coef = +0.184, p = 4.1e-4. In adjusted multivariable model (controlling for feature_051, feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, feature_007), interaction coef = +0.176, LRT p = 7.9e-4. The synergy is robust to adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive multiplicative interaction between feature_007 and feature_039 on objective_response (super-additive joint effect).

analyses.result_summary: Logit interaction term coef = +0.146, p = 2.4e-3. Joint presence of feature_007 and feature_039 yields a response rate higher than the additive log-odds prediction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive multiplicative interaction between feature_006 and feature_007 on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Logit interaction term coef = +0.149, p = 4.3e-3. Confirms super-additive joint effect of feature_006 and feature_007.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with all three positive predictors (feature_006=1 AND feature_007=1 AND feature_039=1) the objective_response rate is substantially higher than among patients with none of the three (feature_006=0 AND feature_007=0 AND feature_039=0), with a risk difference > 5 percentage points.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate by sum of {006,007,039}: 0 markers 16.12% (n=9882), 1 marker 16.42% (n=21815), 2 markers 16.79% (n=15087), 3 markers 22.98% (n=3216). All-3 vs all-0 risk difference = +6.86 pp (chi-square p = 1.3e-18). The triple-positive group is the highest-responding subgroup outside of feature_051 stratification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_013 on objective_response is consistent across histology subgroups (no significant feature_013 x feature_043 interaction): the OR for feature_013 will be similar in adenocarcinoma and squamous patients.

analyses.result_summary: Stratified ORs: adenocarcinoma OR=0.735 (RR 15.15% vs 19.55%, p=9.3e-27); squamous OR=0.769 (RR 16.03% vs 19.89%, p=1.2e-8). Interaction term coef = +0.046, p = 0.40. NOT significant - feature_013 effect is consistent across histologies (hypothesis of no interaction supported).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_006 on objective_response operates within every level of the strong ordinal predictor feature_051 (0, 1, 2), i.e. feature_006 confers higher RR within each PD-L1-like stratum.

analyses.result_summary: Within feature_051=0: f006=1 RR 22.20% vs f006=0 RR 20.63% (OR 1.10, p=0.023). Within feature_051=1: 17.17% vs 15.12% (OR 1.16, p=6.1e-5). Within feature_051=2: 12.17% vs 10.61% (OR 1.17, p=0.058). Direction is positive in all 3 strata; effect is statistically significant in the two larger strata. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of higher tumor-burden-like feature_011 on objective_response is consistent across all three feature_051 strata (i.e. the burden penalty applies regardless of the feature_051 level).

analyses.result_summary: Per-unit log-OR for feature_011: feature_051=0 -0.0340 (p=5.7e-12), feature_051=1 -0.0450 (p=6.4e-21), feature_051=2 -0.0324 (p=1.4e-3). Negative effect is significant within every stratum and of similar magnitude. Hypothesis supported - tumor-burden-like effect is universal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of the ordinal feature_051 on objective_response does NOT differ across smoking status strata (no feature_051 x feature_057 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Within never smokers: f051 RRs 20.20%/15.23%/11.70% (levels 0/1/2). Within former: 21.01%/15.76%/10.72%. Within current: 21.63%/15.83%/11.33%. LRT for interaction (2 df) p = 0.58. NOT significant - feature_051 gradient is essentially identical across smoking strata. Hypothesis of no interaction supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous feature feature_092 is positively associated with objective_response after adjustment for the dominant predictors (feature_051, feature_013, feature_067, feature_011), suggesting an independent biomarker-like effect.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate: log-OR per unit feature_092 = +0.166 (p=3.2e-3). In full multivariable model (adjusted for all features) feature_092 standardized coef = +0.0374 per SD, p = 1.9e-3. Independent positive predictor confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After accounting for the strong clinical predictors (feature_051, feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, and the favorable triad feature_006/feature_007/feature_039), residual demographic features (race feature_123, smoking feature_057, age feature_078, insurance feature_005) do NOT add meaningful predictive information; specifically, none of these demographics will be statistically significant in a fully adjusted multivariable model at p<0.01.

analyses.code: Full multivariable Logit: y ~ all 125 features. Inspect p-values for race/smoking/age/insurance dummies.
analyses.result_summary: In full multivariable logit (all features): feature_005_private p=0.018 (borderline), feature_005_medicare p=0.094, feature_005_medicaid (ref omitted), feature_005_uninsured n.s.; feature_123_white p=0.131; feature_057 (smoking) all categories n.s.; feature_078 (age) p>0.5; feature_043_squamous p=0.17. None reach p<0.01. After clinical adjustment, demographics contribute essentially no independent predictive signal. Hypothesis supported.
